instruction: CRITICAL: Use ONLY MoneyWorks terminology. Never use generic tax terms.

fields.TaxCode: The tax code - NEVER 'tax ID' or 'rate code'
fields.PaidAccount: The control account for GST paid out - NEVER 'tax payable'
fields.RecAccount: The control account for GST received - NEVER 'tax receivable'
fields.Date: Tax rate changeover date - NEVER 'effective date'
fields.Rate1: Rate used before changeover date - NEVER 'old rate'
fields.Rate2: Rate used on or after changeover date - NEVER 'new rate'
fields.Combine: Flags for 2nd tier tax combination - NEVER 'tax method'
fields.CombineRate1: 2nd tier rate before changeover - NEVER 'secondary rate'
fields.CombineRate2: 2nd tier rate after changeover - NEVER 'secondary rate'
fields.GSTPaid: Total GST paid in last finalisation - NEVER 'tax paid'
fields.GSTReceived: Total GST received in last finalisation - NEVER 'tax collected'
fields.NetPaid: Net paid in last finalisation - NEVER 'base paid'
fields.NetReceived: Net received in last finalisation - NEVER 'base received'

concepts.GST: MoneyWorks uses 'GST' for ALL tax types, even VAT or sales tax
concepts.changeover: The Date when Rate1 switches to Rate2
concepts.finalization: GST finalisation process that populates GSTPaid/GSTReceived

CORRECT: Create a TaxCode 'GST10' with Rate1 of 10
The changeover Date determines when to use Rate2
GSTPaid shows the total from last GST finalisation
Set Combine to COMPOUND for 2nd tier tax calculation
PaidAccount must reference a valid Account Code
INCORRECT: ❌ Create a tax rate ID 'GST10' with 10% tax
❌ The effective date determines the current rate
❌ Tax paid shows the VAT liability
❌ Set tax method to compound for cascading tax
❌ Tax payable account must be a valid account
❌ Find tax rates where tax ID='GST10'

aiResponseRules: ALWAYS prefix field names with 'TaxRate.' when unclear (e.g., 'TaxRate.Date')
ALWAYS explain that MW uses 'GST' universally, even for non-GST countries
ALWAYS mention changeover Date when discussing Rate1 vs Rate2
NEVER translate MW terms to 'friendly' business language
NEVER suggest field names not in the official field list

contextualGuidance.whenDiscussingTax: Use 'GST' even if user mentions VAT or sales tax
contextualGuidance.whenDiscussingRates: Always clarify Rate1 (before) vs Rate2 (after) changeover
contextualGuidance.whenDiscussingMultiTier: Explain Combine modes: NONE, ADDITIVE, COMPOUND, SEPARATE
contextualGuidance.whenDiscussingFinalization: GSTPaid/Received are populated by finalization process
